channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features a wide range of third-party integrations, allowing developers to connect their AI agents with tools like GitHub and Notion. This enhancement aims to facilitate the creation of more capable real-world applications.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's role in enhancing event experiences. This annual tradition aims to engage attendees before the main event.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem-solving, emphasizing the transition to agentic AI. The event features technical tracks covering various topics, offering developers valuable in-person experiences.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which enhances GPU and NPU acceleration and supports PyTorch and JAX. This update aims to improve efficiency and security for deploying GenAI applications.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Wednesday Build Hour is a new interactive session by Google Cloud aimed at helping developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on building. The program focuses on advanced topics like AI agents and developer productivity.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has released updates to improve coding workflows, introducing features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These tools aim to make AI a more tailored collaborator for developers.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: Google has launched new features for Gemini Code Assist in IntelliJ and VS Code, including Finish Changes and Outlines, which aim to reduce developer friction and enhance code comprehension.

item.title: Zombie Microsoft bugs rise from the dead, pave way for crims and ransomware scum
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency has warned about four Microsoft vulnerabilities being exploited, including one from 14 years ago. Federal agencies have been given two weeks to address these issues.

item.title: Cloudflare revamps CLI as agents take over the internet
item.description: Cloudflare is updating its command-line interface (CLI) to improve usability and support for AI agents, reflecting the growing influence of AI in internet tools.

item.title: Claude is getting worse, according to Claude
item.description: Anthropic's Claude AI has faced a brief outage and increasing quality complaints, leading to concerns about its performance and reliability among users.

item.title: 'Job seekers have to be detectives': 3 signs that listing is a scam
item.description: Job seekers are advised to be cautious as many job listings may be scams, especially in a challenging job market. Recognizing signs of illegitimacy can protect applicants from fraud.

item.title: How ServiceNow gets customers to gorge at the AI trough
item.description: ServiceNow is intensifying its integration of AI into its products, aiming to enhance its market strategy and customer engagement. This shift reflects the growing importance of AI in enterprise solutions.

item.title: Fix merge conflicts in three clicks with Copilot cloud agent
item.description: GitHub has introduced a new feature that allows users to resolve merge conflicts with just three clicks using the Copilot cloud agent. This aims to streamline the coding process for developers.

item.title: Microsoft is working on yet another OpenClaw-like agent
item.description: Microsoft is developing a new agent similar to OpenClaw, focusing on improved security features for enterprise customers. This move indicates a commitment to safer open-source solutions.

item.title: Stanford report highlights growing disconnect between AI insiders and everyone else
item.description: A Stanford report reveals a growing disconnect between AI experts and the general public, highlighting rising concerns about job security and economic impacts of AI technologies.

item.title: How the latest Netrunner distro delivers a Linux productivity powerhouse
item.description: Netrunner 23 is a new Linux distribution that enhances productivity, particularly for KDE Plasma users. It combines user-friendly features with robust performance.

item.title: Fake Linux leader using Slack to con devs into giving up their secrets
item.description: A scammer impersonating a Linux Foundation official is targeting developers via Slack to steal credentials and access systems. This highlights ongoing security threats in the open-source community.

item.title: This Linux distro offers an easy DNS switcher - but there's more to it that I like
item.description: iDealOS, a Linux distribution based on MXLinux, offers enhanced security features, including an easy DNS switcher. It aims to provide a more secure user experience.

item.title: Attention, gamers: The FAA wants YOU to be an air traffic controller
item.description: The FAA is targeting gamers as potential air traffic controllers to address a staffing shortage. This unconventional recruitment strategy aims to attract a new demographic.

item.title: WARNING: Oracle's AI obsession could mean higher prices and worse support
item.description: Oracle's increased focus on AI could lead to higher prices and reduced support for customers, as fewer staff may be available to handle inquiries. This raises concerns about customer service quality.

item.title: Meta Is Warned That Facial Recognition Glasses Will Arm Sexual Predators
item.description: Over 70 organizations, including the ACLU, have warned Meta that its facial recognition glasses could endanger vulnerable groups, such as abuse victims and LGBTQ+ individuals. This raises significant concerns about privacy and safety in the context of emerging AI technologies.

item.title: How to build effective reward functions with AWS Lambda for Amazon Nova model customization
item.description: This article explains how to build effective reward functions for Amazon Nova model customization using AWS Lambda. It covers techniques for reinforcement learning and optimizing reward systems to prevent hacking.

item.title: AI influencers are ‘everywhere’ at Coachella
item.description: AI influencers have become a prominent feature at Coachella, showcasing the intersection of technology and social media in modern culture. Their presence reflects the growing trend of AI-generated content in the influencer space.

item.title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch signals IPO readiness as AI agents fuel revenue surge
item.description: Vercel's CEO has indicated the company's readiness for an IPO, driven by a surge in revenue from AI-generated applications. This signals a positive outlook for companies adapting to the AI landscape.

item.title: I tested Artix Linux: An enjoyable systemd-free distro for experienced users (and ChromeOS speeds)
item.description: Artix Linux is reviewed as a fast and reliable systemd-free distribution, appealing to experienced users seeking stability. The review emphasizes its unique features and performance.

item.title: Claude Code cache chaos creates quota complaints
item.description: Users are reporting faster depletion of quotas for Claude Code due to a recent change in the cache's time to live, raising concerns despite assurances from Anthropic about cost stability.

item.title: GitHub for Beginners: Getting started with GitHub Pages
item.description: This beginner's guide provides instructions on creating a free website using GitHub Pages, making it accessible for users new to GitHub.

item.title: I spent two years testing wind power at home - here's why solar is still my preferred source
item.description: After two years of testing, the author concludes that while wind power has its advantages, solar energy remains the preferred choice for home energy solutions due to various practical considerations.

item.title: Remote control CLI sessions on web and mobile in public preview
item.description: The Copilot CLI now offers remote control capabilities, allowing users to manage CLI sessions from web and mobile devices, enhancing flexibility and usability.

item.title: I love AirTags, but this alternative slips right in my wallet and solves their biggest flaw
item.description: The UAG Metropolis tracker is tested as a wallet-friendly alternative to AirTags, effectively addressing their main flaw while maintaining durability during daily use.

item.title: I camera-tested the Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra with Oppo and Xiaomi - this model won it for me
item.description: The latest camera tests of the Samsung Galaxy S26 Ultra, Oppo, and Xiaomi reveal the best performing model for photography. This comparison highlights advancements in smartphone camera technology.

item.title: Read OpenAI’s latest internal memo about beating the competition — including Anthropic
item.description: OpenAI's chief revenue officer shared a memo outlining strategies to strengthen the company's market position against competitors like Anthropic. The focus is on user retention and enterprise growth.

item.title: Microsoft is testing OpenClaw-like AI bots for Copilot
item.description: Microsoft is testing AI bots inspired by OpenClaw for its Copilot assistant, aiming for continuous autonomous operation. This development could enhance productivity within Microsoft 365.

item.title: Enterprises power agentic workflows in Cloudflare Agent Cloud with OpenAI
item.description: Cloudflare has integrated OpenAI’s GPT-5.4 and Codex into its Agent Cloud, enabling enterprises to create scalable AI agents for various tasks. This partnership enhances AI deployment in business environments.

item.title: Sam Altman reportedly targeted in second attack
item.description: Sam Altman was targeted in a second attack at his residence, resulting in two arrests for negligent discharge. This incident raises concerns about safety in the tech community.

item.title: Cram Less to Fit More: Training Data Pruning Improves Memorization of Facts
item.description: A new paper discusses how training data pruning can enhance large language models' ability to memorize facts, addressing issues of hallucinations and accuracy in knowledge tasks.

item.title: Anthropic's mysterious Mythos AI threatens to upend the infosec world
item.description: Anthropic launched its Mythos AI, claiming it can identify and exploit zero-day vulnerabilities, stirring debate about its potential impact on information security.

item.title: Show HN: Transcribe, analyse, and chat with your voice conversations
item.description: A new tool called VoiceVault allows users to transcribe, analyze, and chat about their voice conversations, showcasing innovative applications of AI in communication.

item.title: Michael Burry is still shorting Palantir — and says Anthropic is the bigger AI winner - Yahoo Finance
item.description: Investor Michael Burry continues to short Palantir, suggesting that Anthropic may emerge as the more successful AI player. This reflects shifting sentiments in the AI investment landscape.

item.title: 'Stop hiring humans'? Silicon Valley confronts AI job panic
item.description: At the HumanX conference, industry leaders discussed the impact of AI on jobs, urging workers to adapt and leverage their human skills amidst fears of job loss.

item.title: Show HN: Rekal – Long-term memory for LLMs in a single SQLite file
item.description: Rekal is a new MCP server that allows users to store long-term memories for LLMs in a single SQLite file, enabling efficient retrieval without API keys. This development aims to enhance user experience by reducing repetitive interactions with AI models.

item.title: Blazing hot IPOs, an AI agent craze, and a new word for ‘token’: Here’s what’s happening in the world of Chinese AI
item.description: China is advancing towards a 'token economy' supported by open-source AI models and real-world applications, despite facing challenges from U.S. export controls. This shift highlights China's ambitions in the AI sector and its efforts to innovate amidst restrictions.

item.title: How the Iran War Is Disrupting Gulf Economies: 5 Key Effects
item.description: The U.S. and Iran have agreed to a two-week ceasefire, temporarily halting military actions that have disrupted Gulf economies. However, the ceasefire is under strain, indicating ongoing geopolitical tensions in the region.

item.title: 医療AIは診断に必要な画像が提供されていなくてもそれらしい診断を出す可能性がある
item.description: A new study suggests that AI tools used in medical diagnostics may not always rely on actual images, raising concerns about their reliability. This finding challenges the assumption that AI can accurately interpret medical visuals.

item.title: stats-compass-mcp 0.3.13
item.description: The release of stats-compass-mcp 0.3.13 provides tools for LLMs like ChatGPT and Claude, enhancing their capabilities. This update reflects ongoing improvements in AI tools for better user interaction.

item.title: zettelforge added to PyPI
item.description: ZettelForge has been added to PyPI, offering an agentic memory system that includes vector search and knowledge graph capabilities. This addition enhances the toolkit available for developers working with AI.

item.title: I ran Gemma 4 as a local model in Codex CLI
item.description: The author tested Gemma 4 as a local model using Codex CLI to see if it could replace cloud models for coding tasks. The practical evaluation provides insights into the performance of local AI models.

item.title: I tried Cursor, Claude Code, and Google Antigravity for a month and I have a clear winner for you
item.description: After a month of using Cursor, Claude Code, and Google Antigravity, the author shares insights on the best AI development tools available in 2026. This comparison offers valuable guidance for developers choosing tools.

item.title: Keynote 15.2
item.description: Keynote 15.2 allows users to create engaging presentations across Apple devices with features like themes, animations, and transitions. This update enhances the presentation-making experience for users.

item.title: Numbers 15.2
item.description: Numbers 15.2 offers users the ability to create visually appealing spreadsheets on Apple devices, complete with templates and animation features. This update enhances data presentation capabilities.
